title: phormium flavovirens
intro: Phormium flavovirens, also known as the Yellow form of Flax Lily, is a perennial plant species native to New Zealand, belonging to the Asphodelaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Phormium flavovirens include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Phormium flavovirens can be made into a salve or cream to apply topically, or infused into a tea for internal consumption, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
